Welcome to the 
Year Ahead
Dear reader, 
2025 follows the ‘Year of the Ballot Box’. More than half the world’s population headed 
to the polls in 2024, and many incumbent governments fared badly as still-high prices 
and interest rates weighed on living standards. 
We wrote in our Year Ahead 2024 that ‘fiscal forever’ was on the ballot. As we enter 
2025, it is no longer just a campaign promise but the newly-elected reality. Government

Dearest, loveliest reader, 
Trend reports tend to be clear and straightforward. The world is messy,
trends are complex and people and markets behave in unexpected ways,
but trend reports are soothing beacons, pointing towards one or more
possible futures. They are meant to reassure us: “don’t worry, I’ll tell you
all the important stuff, so you’ll know what to do next”.
This trend report, however, is not meant to reassure you. Its theme is that
it has no theme. Its theme is that we don’t know what the future is. That
we need to be prepared for whatever. That we need to expect the
unexpected and unexpect the expected.
Trends are evolving so fast now that the moment that I stop writing this
and send it to the graphic designer, about 10% of this content will have
become irrelevant. A number which will only go up as time ticks by. (So
read on quickly, is my humble advice.)
I may be (slightly
acceleration of technology as with generative AI and we’re probably

FOREWORD
[ SOCIAL MEDIA TRENDS 2024 ]
2024 marks the 30th anniversary of Italy’s Roberto Baggio missing a penalty winner against Brazil in the 1994 World Cup 
finals. During the event’s opening ceremony, the American organizers made Diana Ross take a spot kick, which she 
ominously missed. That moment in time was a well-documented cluster bomb in culture, exploding across traditional 
media. Imagine social and memes were available at scale back then. 
Fast-forward to today. Culture is less top-down. Social has enabled a more bottom-up, bubble-up, decentralized way for 
culture to emerge. And the energy, velocity a
